overview.current_phase: Week 5: Email Triage Live + Expo Video Delivered
overview.phase_description: Email triage system built, tested, and ready for go-live. Chuck's Outlook OAuth completes at the Week 5 call — first live digest delivers at 6 AM the following morning. Trade show commercial video nearly complete for the April 28 Tampa expo.
overview.tagline: Your AI Business Operating System — Designed, Installed, Managed.

items.label: SOP structure & documentation
items.note: Chuck wrote all SOPs himself — needs attorney review via Paychex. Policy gaps identified.

items.label: Company playbooks
items.note: Training videos (AI-based) being created by Chuck to eliminate telephone effect. Retraining older crew now.

items.label: Internal knowledge base
items.note: Information siloing is #1 pain — estimators, PMs, accounting all on separate platforms. Trimble Connect will centralize.

items.label: Training documentation
items.note: AI video series active. OSHA 10 → safety gear → technical training. Retraining older crew in progress.

items.label: Decision support tools
items.note: Chuck tried Trimble AI layer live on call to find avg contract size — it failed. This is the core brain gap.

items.label: Brain installation
items.note: Live at brain.allsouthlightningprotection.com. HTTPS secured. Chuck and Morgan have accounts. 164 knowledge chunks across 10 business context files. Used live Week 4 — surfaced personnel backup gaps and structural blind spots. Staff directory current at 49 employees.

items.label: SOP digital system
items.note: Field staff on paper SOPs only — legal and continuity risk. Digital queryable SOP system identified as near-term build. SOPs to start loading into Brain Week 5.

items.label: Staff directory & SOP library
items.note: 45-person staff directory with roles loaded into Brain. 15 SOPs and policies loaded and queryable. Ask the Brain: 'Who handles scheduling?' or 'What is the cellphone policy?'

sops.title: Cellphone Use Policy
sops.summary: Phones on silent during meetings. Personal calls limited during work hours. No phone use while driving company vehicles — pull over or use Bluetooth.

sops.title: Vehicle Accident Investigation Report
sops.file: All South Lightning Protection Vehicle Accident Investigation Report.docx
sops.summary: Form for documenting vehicle accidents involving company vehicles — driver info, incident details, investigation findings.

sops.title: Attendance & Leave Guide
sops.file: Attendance and Leave Guide For Employees.docx
sops.summary: FLSA-compliant attendance and leave policy for non-exempt staff. Covers compensable hours, overtime, leave procedures.

sops.title: Company Vehicle Safety Program
sops.summary: Driver eligibility, safe operation requirements, Florida traffic law compliance. Employees sign acknowledgment form.

sops.title: Drug & Alcohol Policy
sops.file: Drug and Alchohol Policy - Awaiting Approval.docx
sops.summary: Drug-free workplace policy. Covers employees in safety-sensitive roles — field crew, vehicle operators, heavy equipment. Dated 04-07-2026, awaiting approval.

sops.title: Employee Safety Violation & Corrective Action Report
sops.summary: Formal documentation form for safety violations on job sites. Captures incident details, employee and supervisor info, corrective action taken.

sops.title: Failed Lightning Protection System – Corrective Action Form
sops.summary: Used when a project fails inspection (UL, internal, customer, or other). Documents required return visit and corrective actions.

sops.title: Failed UL Certification Report
sops.summary: Employee counseling form tied to a failed UL certification. Signed by employee, supervisor, and HR.

sops.title: Firearms Policy
sops.summary: Firearms not permitted on ASLP property or in company vehicles unless secured in locked storage. Dated 04-07-2026, awaiting approval.

sops.title: GPS & Company Driving Policy
sops.file: GPS Notification and Company Driving Policy.docx
sops.summary: GPS telematics (Verizon Reveal) being evaluated for all company vehicles. Policy covers tracking, fuel efficiency, driver safety, and utilization.

sops.title: Job Roles, Criteria & Advancement
sops.summary: 90-day probation for all new hires. Advancement criteria by role. OSHA 10 required. All promotions subject to availability and business need. Excludes COLA adjustments.

sops.title: New Hire Tool List
sops.summary: Personal tools each new hire must purchase: boots, ratchet set, vice grips, hammer, tape measure, cable cutters, level, allen keys. Company provides PPE and safety harness equipment.

sops.title: Safety Equipment Assignment Sheet
sops.summary: Tracks issuance of personal fall protection equipment per employee: lifeline, SRL, harness, lanyard, rope grab. Includes serial numbers and dates.

sops.title: Day Off / Vacation Request Form
sops.summary: Standard form for requesting time off. Captures employee name, start date, return date, and reason.

sops.title: Warehouse Associate Duties
sops.summary: Covers property maintenance, inventory organization, order fulfillment, material delivery, fabrication, and equipment maintenance.

items.label: Lead generation mapping
items.note: Electricians (#1 referral source), trade shows, on-site pre-bids, Google SEO + Ads. ~300 active jobs across 3 locations.

items.label: Lead intake mapping
items.note: Joe answers main line, routes to Jeff/Morgan (estimating) or Ron (sales). Leads also bypass to Charley/Giovanni via text/email. Admin staff discouraged from technical questions but sometimes answer — causes issues.

items.label: Quoting process mapping
items.note: Jeff reviews up to 1,000 pages of architect drawings + spec books per job manually. ~1 week turnaround. Quote = final letter only. Massive AI opportunity for document analysis.

items.label: Scheduling mapping
items.note: Giovanni is scheduler. Overwhelmed by volume + constant field changes. Uses email + Outlook calendar. Sometimes denies customer requests rather than problem-solving — damaging customer confidence.

items.label: Follow-up mapping
items.note: Post-job follow-up not systematized. Review requests happen but not consistently.

items.label: Retention mapping
items.note: Monday.com annual email system ineffective — GC/electrician hires don't share building owner contact. QR sticker on electrical panels is workaround but not in closeout checklist. Inspection dept growth is long-term vision.

items.label: Automation opportunities identified
items.note: Week 2 confirmed: email triage selected as #1 priority. Also identified: call transcription, smart scheduler, field crew photo gate, daily brief.

items.label: Voice/transcription agent
items.note: Chuck wants phone call transcription for hardlines. Otter tried but removed — too aggressive, sent itself to customers. Whisper/voice-to-text flagged as solution.

items.label: CRM workflows
items.note: Trimble Connect rolling out — will be the CRM backbone. Automation to layer on top once live.

items.label: Performance reporting
items.note: Chuck drones job sites manually ~1x/week to spot-check crew accountability. No reporting loop.

quick_wins.title: Email Triage System
quick_wins.description: Chuck's Outlook (chuck@aslp.net) — 50+ emails daily. System reads inbox each morning, categorizes into Action Required / FYI / Bulk Noise, and delivers a single prioritized daily digest by 6:00 AM. Built and tested. Going live at the Week 5 call — Chuck authorizes his Outlook account and the schedule activates. Crystal's inbox is Phase 2.
quick_wins.impact: Eliminates 1–2 hours of daily inbox paralysis. Delivers one clear action list every morning before the crew heads out.

quick_wins.title: Trade Show Commercial Video
quick_wins.description: AI-produced commercial video for the Tampa construction trade show (April 28, 2026). Designed for trade show booth display — looping format with captions for a loud expo floor environment. Showcases All South's commercial lightning protection work and capabilities.
quick_wins.impact: Professional brand presence at the April 28 Tampa expo. No production crew needed — delivered via the AI OS program.

quick_wins.title: Phone Call Transcription
quick_wins.description: Calls transcribed automatically → organized into lead file → moves to job file. Solves costly disputes when customers claim job details differ from agreement. Otter removed for being too aggressive.
quick_wins.impact: Complete call-to-job record. Eliminates 'he said she said' disputes.

quick_wins.title: Smart Scheduler
quick_wins.description: Giovanni (scheduler) overwhelmed — uses email + Outlook, sometimes denies customer requests. Smart scheduler looks at overall workload, rearranges tasks/personnel to accommodate new requests without overcommitting.
quick_wins.impact: Stops revenue loss from poor scheduling decisions. Restores customer confidence.

quick_wins.title: Field Crew Photo Gate
quick_wins.description: Missing photo evidence can require removing concrete to prove work was done. Proposed: a gate system that blocks timesheet submission until required photos are uploaded.
quick_wins.impact: Eliminates legal/cost exposure from missing job site documentation.

quick_wins.title: Customer Retention Touchpoint
quick_wins.description: Monday.com annual email ineffective — GC hires don't share building owner contact. QR sticker on panels is workaround but not in closeout checklist. Need: sticker added to mandatory closeout gate + tracking system.
quick_wins.impact: Grows inspection department — long-term high-margin revenue stream.

quick_wins.title: Automated Quote Analysis
quick_wins.description: Jeff manually reviews up to 1,000 pages of architect drawings and spec books per quote. AI system could digitally process documents and extract required data to dramatically accelerate quoting.
quick_wins.impact: Saves hours per quote across thousands of jobs per year. Massive capacity unlock.

pain_points: Information siloing — estimators, PMs, accounting all on separate platforms. Data retrieval is 'like pulling teeth.'
Field crew documentation — constant chasing for photos and job updates despite simple process
30–50 emails per morning — manual triage consuming Chuck's first 1–2 hours daily
JJ (PM) is very new and under pressure — Chuck checks in daily
Retention system decommissioned with no replacement
Office typing speed avg 30 WPM (vs 60-80 standard) — voice-to-text opportunity
Chuck drones job sites manually ~1x/week to spot-check crew — no reporting system
Otter tried and failed — too invasive, inserted itself into customer systems
Trimble AI layer tested live on call — couldn't return avg contract size in real-time
tech_stack: Trimble Connect (project management) — rolling out, replacing monday.com
Track Spera (timesheets) — planned, still on paper
Microsoft Suite — Outlook, Teams, Excel, Word — daily
ChatGPT — daily AI queries and document drafting
On-site server — managed by 3rd party IT
key_discoveries.internal_sell_challenge: 30-year 'handshake and dollar sign' culture. Ron and leadership built the business on relationships alone. AI must be introduced through results — never called 'AI' upfront. Chuck is the internal champion navigating this carefully.
key_discoveries.relationship_note: Chuck said verbatim: 'I trust you with my family.' This is a high-trust personal relationship, not just a business transaction. Reciprocate accordingly.

sessions.title: Week 1: Full Business Discovery
topics: Business overview — what All South does and day-to-day operations
Revenue model — $13M projected 2026, 90% repeat business, avg $20-30K contract
Employee structure — ~300 total across 3 locations (Tampa HQ, Sunrise/Miami, Jacksonville)
Training program — AI video series built by Chuck to eliminate telephone effect; OSHA 10; retraining older crew now
SOP & documentation inventory — full form library, new hire manual; all policies need attorney review
Technology stack — Trimble suite (mid-transition), monday.com being retired, ChatGPT daily, Otter tried/removed
Key pain points — information siloing, field crew documentation, 30-50 emails/day, retention gap
Retention system — annual inspection automation being decommissioned, no replacement identified
Redundancy & succession — double-trained system; JJ is very new PM, under pressure
Revenue targets — $13M 2026; $15-20M 3yr; $50M long-term vision
Next steps — Thursday recurring meeting, contract + invoice to be sent, ZooTampa press release

sessions.title: Week 2: Business Process Mapping
topics: SOP inventory — confirmed existing policies: accident reporting, vehicle use, cell phone, attendance/PTO, GPS, certifications roadmap, equipment sign-out, PM performance evals
Gaps identified: field crew photo SOP (unwritten), QR sticker not in mandatory closeout checklist
Server organization — Tampa only, branches excluded. Some docs on personal devices. Centralization is priority.
Lead intake mapped — Joe routes calls to Jeff/Morgan/Ron. Charley + Giovanni also receive leads directly via text/email.
Quoting process — Jeff reviews 1,000-page document packages manually. 1-week turnaround. AI opportunity flagged.
Retention system — Monday.com email ineffective (no building owner contact from GC hires). QR sticker workaround in place.
Scheduling gap — Giovanni overwhelmed, using Outlook. Sometimes denying customer requests. Smart scheduler requested.
Automation pick confirmed: Email triage (#1) — 50 emails/day, 254 unread, wants daily actionable checklist
Call transcription confirmed as #2 priority
Daily brief / hub concept validated
Expo commercial video — visuals needed in Dropbox. Silent loop or captioned voiceover for trade show environment.
Next session: Thursday April 13 — Brain demo + expo video planning. Ron included in future website sessions.
